prerequisites: A Perigon account. Sign up at https://www.perigon.io/sign-up and verify your email, or log in if you already have a Perigon account.
A Perigon API key created from the developer section of your Perigon account.
A start_date value that marks the earliest date from which to retrieve data.

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: Perigon requires an API key on every request, supplied through the connector's api_key secret field. Perigon accepts the key as the x-api-key header, as an Authorization: Bearer token, or as an apiKey query parameter. Requests without a valid key return 401 Unauthorized.

setupSteps.title: Create a Perigon account
setupSteps.body: Go to https://www.perigon.io/sign-up and create an account, then verify your email. If you already have a Perigon account, log in at https://www.perigon.io instead.

setupSteps.title: Generate an API key
setupSteps.body: Open the developer section of your Perigon account and create an API key. Keep this key secure and do not share it, since it authenticates every request.

setupSteps.title: Provide the api_key
setupSteps.body: Enter the generated key in the connector's api_key field. The connector sends it to authenticate calls to the Perigon API; an invalid or missing key results in a 401 Unauthorized response.

setupSteps.title: Set the start_date
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date field to the earliest date from which the connector should pull records.
